query: Voyager 1 Gliese 445 closest approach estimated years trajectory

mako.raw:
{"query":"Voyager 1 Gliese 445 closest approach estimated years trajectory","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1262,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://mathscinotes.wordpress.com/2013/06/30/voyager-1-and-gliese-445/","title":"Voyager 1 and Gliese 445 | Math Encounters Blog","content":"Jun 30, 2013 — Voyager 1 is moving toward the star Gliese 445 ・ be at the point of closest approach in about 40,000 years The closest Gliese 445 and Voyager ...
Voyager 1 is moving toward the star Gliese 445 (aka AC+79 3888) and will be at the point of closest approach in about 40,000 years
I compute that the time of closest approach is 46,000 years from now, which is close to the Wikipedia statement of “about 40,000 years“.
I compute the distance at closest approach as 3.485 light-years, which is close to the Wikipedia value of 3.45 light-years."},{"url":"https://www.iflscience.com/in-40000-years-voyager-1-will-have-a-close-encounter-with-gliese-445-80755","title":"In 40,000 Years, Voyager 1 Will Have A Close Encounter With Gliese 445 | IFLScience","content":"Sep 10, 2025 — Voyager 1 will actually get within 1.7 light years of AC +79 3888 (aka Gliese 445) in 40,000 years." happens it will be around 3.5 light-years ...
In 40,000 years, long after its mission is over, NASA's Voyager 1 spacecraft will have a close encounter with Gliese 445.
Assuming that the Voyager probes make it through the cloud undamaged (a likely outcome, given that space is not the asteroid-dodging exercise sci-fi would have us believe), they could go on relatively unscathed for many, many years beyond that.
"It took 35 years to reach interstellar space, but it will take 40,000 years for Voyager 1 to be closer to the star AC +79 3888 than our sun," NASA explains.
They found that the spacecraft will encounter TYC 3135-52-1, a main-sequence star, in around 303,000 years."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1","title":"Voyager 1 - Wikipedia","content":"Voyager 1's initial orbit had an aphelion of 8.9 AU (830 million mi), just a little short of Saturn's orbit of 9.5 AU (880 million mi).
Its closest approach to Jupiter was on March 5, 1979, at a distance of about 349,000 kilometers (217,000 miles) from the planet's center.
Voyager 1 finished photographing the Jovian system in April 1979.
Media related to the Voyager 1 Jupiter encounter at Wikimedia Commons
Voyager 1 encountered Saturn in November 1980, with the closest approach on November 12, 1980, when the space probe came within 124,000 kilometers (77,000 mi) of Saturn's cloud-tops.
The trajectory Voyager 1 was launched into would not have allowed it to continue on to Uranus and Neptune,: 155 but could have been altered to avoid a Titan flyby and travel from Saturn to Pluto, arriving in 1986.
As of late April 2026, two instruments remain in operation.
Though it is not heading towards any particular star, in about 40,000 years, it will pass within 1.6 light-years (0.49 parsecs; 100,000 astronomical units) of the star Gliese 445, which is in the constellation Camelopardalis and 17.1 light-years from Earth (as of 2010).
In 300,000 years, it will pass within less than 1 light-year of the M3V star TYC 3135–52–1."},{"url":"https://www.wionews.com/photos/in-40-000-years-voyager-1-will-brush-past-gliese-445-understanding-the-star-of-camelopardalis-1756938570662","title":"In 40,000 years, Voyager 1 will brush past Gliese 445! Understanding the star of Camelopardalis","content":"Sep 4, 2025 — Voyager 1, launched in 1977, is on a trajectory that will take it near the star Gliese 445 in about 40,000 years. Gliese 445 is a red dwarf ...
Gliese 445 is a red dwarf located roughly 17 light years away.
If Voyager were still functional, it could measure the star’s stellar wind, magnetic field, and any surrounding plasma environment."},{"url":"https://www.centauri-dreams.org/2015/05/06/thoughts-on-voyagers-closest-stars/","title":"Thoughts on Voyager’s Closest Stars | Centauri Dreams","content":"But Gliese 445 is another kind of target.
It’s moving in our direction at roughly 120 kilometers per second, so that at its closest approach it will be 3.485 light years out, closer than Proxima Centauri is today.
Voyager 2, meanwhile, should pass within 111,000 AU of HH Andromedae, otherwise known as Ross 248, in the same 40,000 years.
Also moving in our direction, Ross 248 becomes the closest star to the Sun in the 40,000 year time frame considered here."}]}
